failure_modes.text: Alignment metrics can hide real divergence when dashboards or formal compliance are mistaken for actual goal alignment.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Alignment Metrics Hide Real Behavior: The model is misused when principals mistake dashboards, formal compliance, or reported activity for true goal alignment while the agent still benefits from divergence underneath.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit alignment beyond dashboards: periodically verify that formal metrics still track the principal's real goal, and sunset metrics that have become performative.

failure_modes.text: Micromanagement can treat the case-at-hand while leaving the machine-level design error untouched.
failure_modes.source_quote: This results in micromanagement, fails to build the Agent's long-term capacity, and prevents continuous system improvement.
failure_modes.mitigation: Address the system, not the symptom: when misalignment surfaces, redesign incentives, information flows, or role boundaries rather than adding supervision layers.

failure_modes.text: Diagnosis can go wrong when leaders confuse capability failure with capacity, system, or incentive failure.
failure_modes.source_quote: When diagnosing a failure, the Principal confuses a poor outcome due to bad system design or lack of resources (capacity issue) with the Agent's personal flaws or incompetence (capability issue).
failure_modes.mitigation: Diagnose before blaming: distinguish whether poor agent performance stems from misaligned incentives, insufficient capability, inadequate resources, or bad system design.

premortem_questions.text: If this outcome is bad, is the responsible party incapable and/or is the design bad?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this outcome is bad, is the Responsible Party incapable and/or is the design bad?

heuristics.text: Define roles, decision boundaries, and expectations clearly enough that delegated work stays legible to the principal's actual objective.
heuristics.source_quote: The Agent's role explicitly defines what they are supposed to do and how they fit into the larger architecture.

heuristics.text: Diagnose drift sequentially by separating goals, problems, root causes, design changes, and execution instead of blurring them together.
heuristics.source_quote: The Principal must execute these steps sequentially without blurring them.

heuristics.text: Spell out what would have to be true for the current delegated setup to work, so hidden alignment assumptions become inspectable.
heuristics.source_quote: It involves spelling out all the implicit assumptions underlying a proposal or perspective, forcing clarity and rigor in thinking.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on delegated-objective drift, machine-level diagnosis, and role-boundary clarity for alignment repair.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor multi-agent orchestration sprawl because the raw source is stronger on alignment, accountability, and hidden-behavior pressure.
open_questions: The first heuristic compresses both organizational and AI-agent role-definition passages into one reusable alignment move.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on incentives, information asymmetry, and systems-thinking for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on moral-hazard-style hidden divergence and frame substitution by overloaded agents.
structured_tensions: The source includes an explicit structured tension block around moral-hazard and information-asymmetry that cleanly maps into the current relation layer vocabulary.
